name: Last Known Position Search
description: An AI search behavior that investigates where the player was last seen or heard after contact is lost.
design_notes: Last-known-position search makes stealth recovery more believable than instant forgetfulness. It should define memory decay, search pattern, communication to allies, and when AI returns to patrol.
design_purpose: Let players break line of sight without instantly resetting danger.
Create readable search pressure after detection.
Bridge chase and patrol states with believable investigation.
player_fantasy: The player feels hunted but able to outsmart searchers by relocating and hiding.

parameters.name: memory_confidence_decay
parameters.description: Decay applied to confidence in the last-known position.

parameters.name: search_radius_growth_per_second
parameters.description: Expansion of search area while the player remains unseen.

parameters.name: search_waypoint_count
parameters.description: Number of positions sampled during the search pattern.

parameters.name: patrol_resume_confidence
parameters.description: Confidence threshold below which AI returns to patrol.

edge_cases: The player entering a vent, elevator, or water should update or hide search clues consistently.
Multiple AI sharing memory can create unfair perfect knowledge.
Search should not path into unreachable positions caused by navmesh gaps.
common_bugs: AI searches the player's current hidden position because memory stores a live transform reference.
Confidence never decays after losing line of sight.
Returning to patrol clears alert state before allies finish searching.
balancing_notes: Long memory sustains tension; short memory supports forgiving stealth resets.
Search radius should create escape routes, not omniscient coverage.
accessibility_notes: Communicate search state through barks, icons, or posture so players understand they are not fully safe yet.

implementation_notes.unity: Write immutable stimulus records with position, timestamp, confidence, and source into an AI blackboard, then sample NavMesh search points around that stored position.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep remembered_position and confidence on the AI state, generate NavigationAgent targets from reachable points near that memory, and decay confidence during search.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception to update Blackboard keys for last seen/heard location, then run Behavior Tree or StateTree search tasks until confidence reaches the patrol threshold.
implementation_notes.web: Store last-known position as data, not a live player reference, and sample reachable graph nodes around it while confidence decay controls when patrol resumes.

example_games.title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
example_games.note: Uses enemy search behavior after guards lose direct contact with the player.
